12 Stones Global · Kilo Aupuni · govOS · public service

Title 14 — Public Services Water · Plain-Language Service Free · no account

A plain-language navigator to Maui County Code Title 14 (Public Services), Article 1 — Water: how to get water service, how metering and charges work, how to pay your bill, and who pays. Run by the Department of Water Supply (DWS); you manage and pay your account on the County's billing portal — this service explains the law and hands you off to the official system. Usage rates change with the annual budget; figures marked expanding are being filled in. Nothing here is legal advice.

★ Parcel lookup live data

Water service depends on your location. Enter a Maui County TMK to see the parcel's designations from the Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS. Whether DWS can serve a parcel turns on being within service limits and adjacent to a distributing main with adequate supply — confirm a specific parcel's water availability with DWS Customer Service (270-7730).

Tip: type the digits of your TMK (zone-section-plat-parcel). Don't know it? Open the State Land Use Viewer ↗ to find it on a map.

Live designations: Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS. Water service & billing: Dept. of Water Supply. No parcel data is stored or fabricated — fetched live. water service-area overlay

1 · Get water service sourced

How a new or transferred water account works under Ch. 14.04.

  1. Check availability
    DWS may provide service where the premises are within service limits, adjacent to a distributing main, pressure permits, and there is sufficient developed supply for domestic, irrigation, and fire protection without detriment to existing customers.
    Expense: free to ask · paid by: you (time)
  2. Apply / open an account
    Call DWS Customer Service (270-7730, 1888 Wili Pa Loop, Wailuku) to open an account, start/stop service, or change address, and agree to abide by the County water code.
    Expense: account setup · paid by: applicant
  3. Meter & connection
    All water is metered in gallons (a flat rate may apply where metering is impractical). DWS sets the location and size of meters and service connections; connections become DWS property for operation and maintenance.
    Expense: meter/connection fees · paid by: applicant
  4. System improvements (if needed)
    Where system improvements aren't yet installed, the applicant must install and pay for the required storage tanks, appurtenances, and pipeline from the storage tank to the premises (Ch. 14.05).
    Expense: storage + pipeline · paid by: applicant
  5. Get billed & pay
    You receive a monthly bill (meter service charge + usage). Pay online, by phone, or via Automatic Bill Payment.
    Expense: monthly water/sewer · paid by: customer
⚖️ Position — James Langford (analysis, not law; a stance beside the rule)Water is life — a public trust resource. Where the County requires an applicant to build public water infrastructure (storage, mains) to get served, that cost-shift should be weighed against the County's duty to provide clean, plentiful water; the burden of extending the public system shouldn't fall entirely on the last resident to connect.

Maui County Code Ch. 14.04 (Water Service) incl. §14.04.060; Ch. 14.05 (improvements). County Department of Water Supply. new-meter & source-development fee schedule

2 · Water charges sourced

Your bill has two parts: a monthly meter Water Service Charge (to read/maintain/replace your meter) plus usage. Usage rates are set in the County's annual budget ordinance.

ChargeAmountNotes
Monthly Water Service Charge — 5/8" meter$9.25smallest residential meter
Monthly Water Service Charge — 8" meter$650.00largest listed
Water usage (per unit)set in annual budgettiered; verify current year
Source development / improvementsvariesapplicant-installed where needed (Ch. 14.05)

County of Maui Water Charges; meter service charges $9.25 (5/8") to $650 (8"); usage per the annual rates & fees ordinance. current tiered usage-rate table

3 · Pay your water bill sourced

Manage and pay your DWS account online.

Create an account to view bills, see account info, and go paperless. Pay online or by phone with credit card, debit card, electronic check, Apple Pay, or Google Pay; or set up Automatic Bill Payment (ABP) so your bank pays the water/sewer bill automatically on the due date. Questions, start/stop service, or address changes: DWS Customer Service 270-7730 (Mon–Fri, 8am–4pm).

County of Maui Pay Your Water Bill + Fiscal/Customer Service Division.

4 · Enforcement sourced

Water-code violations are reachable through the County enforcement engine. Not legal advice.

Title 19's enforcement chapter, §19.530.030, expressly reaches violations of MCC titles 8, 12, 14, 16, 18, 19 and 20 plus related rules and permits — so a Title 14 water violation (e.g. unauthorized connection or cross-connection) can draw a notice of violation and order with civil fines, with one carve-out the code itself names: unpaid civil fines may be added to County taxes or charges except residential water/sewer. DWS also enforces its own service rules directly.

Maui County Code Ch. 19.530 (§19.530.030 reach + the residential water/sewer carve-out, sourced in the Title 19 service) + DWS service rules. Title 14-specific penalty provisions

5 · Who pays — the charter lens analysis

Labeled analysis — water service read through two charters. Not adopted law; an interpretive lens beside the Code.

Maui County Charter — lensUnder the Maui County Charter, the Department of Water Supply (a semi-autonomous department with its own Board) provides water as a self-supporting utility — rates and fees are set to cover operation, maintenance, and capital, with the Charter directing the County to provide clean, healthful, and plentiful water.
Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) — lensUnder the Sovereign Charter (12 Stones) lens, water is a public-trust resource, not merely a commodity; the resident's claim to domestic water is a foundational right. The lens reads cost-recovery as legitimate for use, but treats forcing the last-connecting resident to fund the public main itself as the County offloading a public duty.
⚖️ Position — analysis, not lawCharge fairly for the water a household uses; treat the backbone system (mains, source, storage) as the County's public duty under its own charge to provide plentiful water. Same service, read two ways.

6 · Rules → Code: candidates for codification analysis

⚖️ Analysis / position — not lawProvisions that, in our assessment, should be codified or made transparent in Title 14 so cost and duty are readable from the Code. Citations are verbatim-checked; the recommendation is a stance beside the rule.
  • Publish the usage-rate table in-Code. Usage rates live in the annual budget ordinance and shift yearly. → Require a plain-language tiered-rate table published with each rate change so residents can see the trend. expanding
  • Source-development cost sharing. Whether the applicant or the County funds new mains/storage is decided case-by-case. → Codify a cost-share rule so extending the public system isn't fully downloaded onto the newest connection.
  • Protect the residential water/sewer carve-out. The Code already shields residential water/sewer from tax-lien collection of fines — keep and clarify that protection in Title 14 itself.

Analysis grounded in MCC Title 14 + the County water rates framework; contents expanding pending verbatim ingest.

Free now (no account): this page, the live parcel lookup, the service navigator, and the charges table are all Tier 1 — free. Coming — Tier 3 (verified tools): a water-service-availability checker and an AI water-account assistant will be verification-gated (identity-verified). Identity is verified through the payment processor — only verified-status is stored here, never the raw ID. No live billing is enabled (Stripe keys are environment references only); the actual water payment happens on the County's own billing portal.
govOS · Kilo Aupuni · 12 Stones Global · free public service · sourced to Maui County Code Title 14 (Public Services), Art. 1 Water via Municode + County of Maui Department of Water Supply + Hawaiʻi Statewide GIS · first version 2026-06-18, expanding · Built by the County Service Module Engine (config + sourced content — no bespoke page) · usage rates set annually — verify with DWS · enforcement reach per MCC §19.530.030 (residential water/sewer carve-out noted) · not legal advice · public records only · hands off to the County's billing portal.
